---
title: "Inspect Debian Packages with `apt-file`, `rmadison`, and `apt-cache rdepends`"
subtitle: "Note#205"
id: "205"
date: 2026-04-15
date-modified: ""
date-format: iso
categories: [notes]
spotlight: ""
---
Which package ships this command? Which version does Debian ship? What depends on this package? Use these commands.

## `apt-file search`

```bash
apt-file search rg
```

Search package contents without installing the package. This is substring matching, so bare command names can be noisy (`-F` does exact matching if you already know the full path -- like `$(command -v <cmd>)`).

To use `apt-file`:

```bash
apt install apt-file
apt-file update
```

## `apt-file show`

```bash
apt-file show ripgrep
```

List the files shipped by a package, even if it is not installed.

## `rmadison`

```bash
rmadison postgresql
```

Show which version of a package is available across Debian suites.

```text
postgresql | 13+225+deb11u1 | oldoldstable | all
postgresql | 15+248+deb12u1 | oldstable    | all
postgresql | 17+278         | stable       | all
postgresql | 18+290         | testing      | all
postgresql | 18+290         | unstable     | all
```

Installed with `apt install devscripts`.

## `apt-cache rdepends`

```bash
apt-cache rdepends git
apt-cache --installed rdepends git
```

Show reverse dependencies from the local APT cache. `--installed` limits the output to installed packages.

## References

* `man apt-file`
* `man apt-cache`
* `man rmadison`
